To automatically establish connections or attachments between components or data without manual intervention.

To change from one thing to another, or to redirect attention or action to a different option.

To employ or apply something for a specific purpose or action.
To keep or retain something in a place for later use or retrieval.
To recognize, determine, or establish what someone or something is.
To automatically discover or identify something without requiring manual intervention.
To catch, fasten, or attach something using a curved or pointed implement, or to integrate with a system at a designated point of extension.
To present or consider something from a different perspective or context.
To make something smooth and shiny through rubbing, or to improve and refine something to a higher standard.
To come to rest on a surface after descending from the air, or to arrive at and settle in a particular place or state.
Make something conform to or establish a uniform set of rules, specifications, or procedures across multiple instances or contexts.
To examine or study something systematically and carefully to establish facts or discover information.
To harvest or gather crops from a field, or more broadly, to obtain or experience something as a consequence of one's actions.
To cease motion or action, or to bring something to an end.

To investigate or examine something carefully and thoroughly.
To cut or shape material, typically wood or stone, using a sharp tool.
To be unsuccessful in completing a task or meeting expectations.

to adjust or customize something to fit specific requirements or circumstances.

To revise or remake something to improve or correct it.
To pledge or dedicate oneself to a course of action, or to deliver code changes to a version control system.
To convert data or text into a different structural arrangement.
To automatically generate or instantiate something without explicit user action.
To decide on and arrange in advance how something will be done or accomplished.
To contain or comprise something as a component, part, or member of a larger whole.

Remove or erase something.
To automatically complete a registration or enrollment process without requiring manual action or input.
To create an identical copy or duplicate of something, particularly a repository or biological organism.
To legally take a child into one's family, or to accept and begin using something formally.
To stop something from happening or to make something impossible.
To pass a thin strand or object through a narrow opening or series of openings, or to navigate skillfully through a confined space.
To bring separate things together into a single whole.
To make or become more firm, secure, or snug.
To furnish with new or replacement parts, equipment, or fittings; to restore or adjust to proper working condition.
To bring something to completion or conclusion.
To create a duplicate copy of data or files for safekeeping and recovery in case of loss or damage.
To restrict or set a maximum boundary on the extent or amount of something.
To execute or operate; to move forward at a faster pace than walking by propelling oneself with alternating leg movements.
To place something upon or over another object, or to traverse and encompass an area or topic.
To make something official, formal, or legally binding.
To make changes or corrections to something after examining it.
To present someone or something to another person, or to bring something new into use or awareness for the first time.
To assign something to a different category or group after an initial assignment.
To calculate or determine a result through mathematical or logical operations.
A modal auxiliary verb expressing obligation, advisability, or expected probability.
To organize or arrange different elements to work together in a harmonious or effective way.
To supply, sell, or provide goods or services commercially to customers or markets.
To direct attention toward something or someone, typically by extending a finger or indicating a direction.
To make or become firm, solid, or more resistant to damage or change.
To examine, verify, or inspect something to determine its condition or accuracy.
To refuse to accept, consider, or believe something.
To secure or fasten something firmly in place, or to serve as a steadying or stabilizing force.
To modify something previously built or installed by adding new or updated components to improve its functionality or meet new requirements.
To mark or damage a surface lightly using fingernails, claws, or a sharp implement.
To return something to its previous or original state or condition.
To fall down or cave in suddenly, or to cause something to do so.
To investigate a subject or topic through systematic examination and study.
To supply or furnish with necessary equipment, resources, or materials.
